target.name: Unspun
target.founder: Beth Esponnette, Walden Lam, Kevin Martin

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $57M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

data.narrative:
Unspun, a Series A robotics and manufacturing company founded by Beth Esponnette, Walden Lam, and Kevin Martin, has raised $57M to date. The valuation assessment draws on a peer universe of nine comparable companies—one public and eight private—operating in the same sector at comparable or adjacent funding stages. Relevant private benchmarks include 1X Technologies, Bedrock Robotics, Dexterity, Collaborative Robotics, and Fairmat, spanning Series B funding rounds.

Two valuation methodologies were applied. Stage-progression analysis, anchoring to the $57M total raised as a floor proxy and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A-to-next-stage advancement, implies a valuation of $114M. Peer-median methodology triangulates across six same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables, yielding a median valuation of $1.1B, with a range spanning $300M to $10.0B. The wide peer dispersion reflects substantial heterogeneity in robotics subsector maturity and application scope.

Triangulation across both methods supports a valuation range of $97M (low) to $1.2B (high), with a median estimate of $594M. The substantial gap between stage-progression and peer-median approaches reflects tension between capital-deployment benchmarks and market multiples observed among more mature robotics ventures. Key uncertainties include the company's relative position within robotics subsectors, path to revenue profitability, and whether peer comparables adequately reflect Unspun's specific technology differentiation and addressable market.
